name: Reaction-Diffusion 3D
_tier: 3D / RENDER — synth3d volume generator. Outputs a 2D volume ATLAS (volumeSize x volumeSize^2) consumed by render/render3d or render/renderLit3d. NOT a raymarch; this is an atlas-WRITE simulation.

uniformLayouts._comment: We bind per-effect uniforms as individual named uniforms (PORTING-GUIDE). speed is declared f32 in the WGSL despite int type in definition.js — bound as float. resetState is boolean (1.0/0.0) read as int and tested != 0.

volumeCache._role: Pooled cache atlas (declared in definition.js; not bound by the simulate pass). volumeSize x volumeSize^2.

geoBuffer._role: outputGeo atlas (xyz=normal, w=depth/SDF). Allocated for downstream lit raymarch; the simulate pass does not write it.

global_rd_state._role: PERSISTENT 3D volume ATLAS + sim state. Per voxel: r=B (density, read by render3d), g/b=visualization colours, a=A (chemical sim state). volumeSize x volumeSize^2 (default 32x1024). Double-buffered/ping-ponged across frames and per repeat:iterations. This IS the outputTex3d.

is3D._comment: synth3d volume tier. The 'volume' is a 2D atlas RenderTexture; depth = volumeSize Z-slices stacked vertically. Atlas mapping: texel (x,y) -> voxel (x, y % volumeSize, y / volumeSize). vol0..7 standard atlas is 64x4096; THIS effect drives the atlas size from the volumeSize param (default 32 -> 32x1024). source/geoSource bind the upstream vol/geo surfaces (inputTex3d/inputGeo).
is3D.atlasMapping: voxel(x, y % volumeSize, y / volumeSize); atlasTexel(p) = (wrap(x), wrap(y) + wrap(z) * volumeSize) with wrap(v)=(v+volumeSize)%volumeSize

feedback._comment: Single-pass feedback sim that WRITES a volume atlas. State lives in the PERSISTENT 'global_' surface rd_state (NOT pooled, NOT '_'-prefixed transient). The runtime double-buffers it: within-frame and per-iteration ping-pong for the repeat:iterations 'simulate' pass (ref 04 §10.6), plus end-of-frame persistence (the pass samples its own previous output via stateTex == global_rd_state). The shader self-initializes on an all-zero buffer and on resetState; no '_iteration' uniform is injected (the shader has no iteration index — it reads `iterations` only to scale the timestep). NO MRT (single color output per pass).
